meta.title: Manuel Heller — Craft Portfolio
meta.description: Full-stack developer with an artist's eye. Code, photo, design: same craft, different surface.

about.headline: Full-stack developer with an artist's eye.
about.subhead: Code, photo, design: same craft, different surface.

parts.heading: Who I am
body: I'm Manuel, full-stack developer at zvoove Switzerland AG since November 2021. PHP and Vue at work, cameras and Photoshop after hours, and everything in between that lives where code meets image. I don't buy the split between 'technical' and 'creative'; the interesting stuff happens exactly in-between.

parts.heading: How I got here
body: Programming came to me through gaming. I set up my own Minecraft modpack server and started writing Mining Turtles in Lua. That was the moment I realised: I want to understand how all of this works under the hood.

My apprenticeship was IT-systems engineer (Betriebsinformatiker EFZ) at Novartis. I actually wanted to be a developer, but Novartis only offered the systems-engineer track, and you don't pass on a Novartis apprenticeship. Four years of servers, networks, support: solid foundation, but by the end it was clear: I'm applying as a developer. With zvoove since 2021, working on the main product zvoove Core, various API/interface tools, and Cypress E2E tests.

parts.heading: How I work with AI
body: AI is like a brush: a tool that doesn't make the art on its own. It's only as good as the plan and the prompt you give it, and it needs someone with taste, creativity, and a clear picture of where this is going. AI-generated websites all look the same today because most people hand the creativity to the AI.

I run a deeply set-up AI workflow: custom agents, hooks, skills, multi-stage prompts, clear convention files. I've taken two complex web apps from zero to production-ready that way (one is the Jogge di Balla platform, the other is this very site). The combination of that workflow depth and the visual sense from photo and design is what ends up making the difference between yet another generic landing page and something you actually remember.

parts.heading: What drives me
body: I get excited about almost everything, and I'm convinced: in the AI era especially you have to stay on it more than ever. Stop learning and you're twelve months away from being left behind. Picking up new skills just feels like fun to me. For me that's not pressure, it's the default mode.

parts.heading: Off the screen
body: Wildlife photography is my biggest passion. I travel solo to Costa Rica or Thailand and trudge into the jungle myself to get animals in the wild in front of the lens. Plus cars (see my Audi S5 tuning project), ski/snowboard, diving, and Jogge di Balla, the club where I'm vice-president, web developer, club photographer, cameraman, and social-media manager all in one.

projects.title: Jogge di Balla
projects.subtitle: Club platform
projects.description: A custom-built all-in-one club platform: from member management to a live Twitch overlay, vibecoded on a modern TypeScript stack.

screenshot.alt: Screenshot of the public homepage of joggediballa.ch
screenshot.caption: joggediballa.ch · home

caseStudy.headline: From club chaos to a custom platform.
caseStudy.hook: All clubs fight the same mess: Excel sheets, static websites, file/info sharing, isolated tools. For Jogge di Balla I cleaned that up: a custom-built platform that covers everything from member management to a Twitch overlay. Vibecoded, self-hosted, over-engineered.

context.label: What is Jogge di Balla?
story: Idea during Covid: four friends, nothing was happening, so we threw oddball events for ourselves. That turned into an actual club with public event classics like Schlag den Kassier, Silvester 2.0, Harassenlauf, and the popular Lotto-Party.

My role: vice-president, web developer, club photographer, cameraman, social-media manager. Because I had the skill set and I'm enough of a perfectionist to give the club not just a face but a custom, self-hosted web app of its own.

items.title: The admin dashboard
items.lede: Invisible to visitors, the heart of the site. Every thread comes together here.
items.screenshotAlt: Admin dashboard with permissions card and member list

features.title: Dynamic permission system
features.body: Granular rights (edit_events, manage_sponsors, edit_shotcounter, …) assignable per card per role, no code changes needed. Backend checks every tRPC call through a custom requirePermission() middleware.

features.title: Attendance tracking with statistics
features.body: Per-event attendance lists, data flows straight into a graphical view. Helps with overview and a bit of internal competition.

features.title: Sponsor lifecycle
features.body: Sponsors managed inside the system. Status (active / expired / new) computed automatically, with finance and address management for our Kassier.

items.title: Schlag den Kassier live overlay
items.lede: Custom Twitch overlay integrated directly into the platform. Instead of expensive external services: just built it myself.
items.screenshotAlt: Twitch overlay during stream with live score and club design

features.title: Hidden URL as browser source
features.body: OBS embeds /overlay/sdk, a control panel at /overlay/sdk/control drives the score live.

features.title: Realtime without page reloads
features.body: tRPC polling syncs the overlay in the stream instantly. Golden shimmer effect for winners, automatic 'mathematically eliminated' calculation.

features.title: Reach: honestly small
features.body: ~15–20 concurrent viewers, ~100 total. The tooling is solidly built anyway, and reusable.

reflection.label: Lessons Learned
reflection.body: Manual testing cost me a lot of time back then. Today I'd set the project up from day one with automated tests and AI tooling, kill bugs at the source, and put the focus entirely on shipping.

photography.headline: Through the Lens.
photography.lede: Wildlife and cars are my two worlds. The first I chase solo through the jungles of Costa Rica or Thailand, the second usually find me closer to home. What connects them: patience, light, and that one moment that justifies the whole day.

koenigsegg.alt: Koenigsegg hypercar under a hotel arch at dusk
koenigsegg.title: Freezed at 30 km/h.
koenigsegg.body: Sometimes the most interesting frame isn't the one with the highest shutter speed. It's the one with the light that only sits there for five minutes.

panorama.alt: Freighters under a dramatic cloud formation on the horizon

treeLake.alt: Lone tree at the edge of a lake, reflection in the water, Costa Rica
treeLake.title: Patience as composition.
treeLake.body: Lake Arenal: an hour and a half waiting for the wind to drop and finally smooth the water. The reflection makes the picture, not the tree.

crocodile.alt: Crocodile with a butterfly on its snout, Costa Rica

pelican.alt: Pelican in flight over the Pacific, Costa Rica

playground.headline: Where the fluid sim gets to play.
playground.lede: Two shader experiments, both anchored to the ink-and-print narrative. Pure craft demonstration, no product context.

inkDropStudio.cardBody: Empty paper canvas, click spawns ink droplets in the chosen spot colour. Sliders for sim parameters, bomb button, freeze button.
inkDropStudio.title: Ink Drop Studio.
inkDropStudio.caption: Playground for the hero simulation. Click = ink splat, sliders = sim parameters, 'Bomb' if you're in the mood for chaos.
inkDropStudio.reducedMotionNote: This experiment is a moving playground. Under prefers-reduced-motion I hide the running simulation. Head back to the Playground for a quieter section.

typeAsFluid.cardBody: Type a word. It gets printed, then swallowed by the ink. Gently.
typeAsFluid.title: Type-as-Fluid.
typeAsFluid.caption: Type a word. It gets printed, then swallowed by the ink. Gently.
typeAsFluid.reducedMotionNote: This experiment prints words live into a running fluid simulation. Under prefers-reduced-motion I hide the animation. Head back to the Playground for a quieter section.

impressum.metaDescription: Imprint and legal information for manuelheller.dev.
impressum.headline: Imprint
impressum.lede: This site is a private, non-commercial portfolio. No paid services are offered.

sections.heading: Responsible for content
body: Manuel Heller
Martisackerweg 18, 4203 Grellingen, Switzerland
manuelheller@bluewin.ch

sections.heading: Nature of this site
body: This site exists solely for self-presentation and showcasing my own work. No products are sold, no paid services are offered, and no advertising is run.

sections.heading: Disclaimer
body: Content is created with care, but without warranty as to completeness or currency. Links and references to external sites are made without responsibility for their content.

sections.heading: Copyright
body: All text, photos, graphics, code samples, and shader source on this site are copyright Manuel Heller unless otherwise marked. Use outside this site requires written permission.

sections.heading: Jurisdiction
body: Swiss law applies. Place of performance and jurisdiction is Basel-Stadt, Switzerland.

datenschutz.metaDescription: Privacy policy for manuelheller.dev. No cookies, no analytics, no tracking.
datenschutz.headline: Privacy policy
datenschutz.lede: Short version: this site sets no cookies, runs no tracking, no analytics. There's no cookie banner because there's nothing that would need consent.

sections.heading: Data controller
body: Manuel Heller
Martisackerweg 18, 4203 Grellingen, Switzerland
manuelheller@bluewin.ch

sections.heading: What data is collected
body: This site sets no cookies. There's no tracking, no analytics, no advertising networks, no social-media pixels. No data is shared with third parties for advertising purposes.

For purely technical reasons the browser's LocalStorage is used: one anonymous entry remembers your device's GPU performance class (so the WebGL animation starts at the right detail level on your next visit) and one entry remembers that the loading screen has already played (so it doesn't replay when you switch language). These values are not personal, are not transmitted to third parties, and can be cleared at any time via your browser settings.

sections.heading: Server logs
body: The hosting server (Linux with nginx) is self-operated and located at the provider mc-host24.de in Germany. DNS resolution runs through Cloudflare (Cloudflare, Inc., USA), which also acts as proxy protection against bot attacks. IP addresses are processed technically in this context but are not stored for tracking purposes. The server logs technically necessary access events: IP address, timestamp, requested URL, referrer, user-agent. These logs are deleted automatically after 14 days and are used exclusively for error analysis and security monitoring (legal basis: legitimate interest, Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR, and Art. 31(2)(b) revFADP).
Cloudflare is covered for data transfers to the USA via Standard Contractual Clauses (Art. 46 GDPR).

sections.heading: Contact form
body: When you use the contact form, your email client's mailto mechanism is currently triggered. The message goes via your email provider, not via a server under my control. Only the data you enter yourself in your mail client is processed.

Should a direct delivery via a server bridge (Resend.com, Resend, Inc., USA) be activated at a later point, your name, email address, and message would be transmitted to me through that technical intermediary. Resend would be covered via Standard Contractual Clauses (Art. 46 GDPR). This statement will be updated accordingly when activated.

Legal basis in both cases: your consent by submitting (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR, Art. 31(1) revFADP). Incoming messages are deleted after they're answered, at the latest after six months. You can request access, correction, or deletion at any time. Just write to manuelheller@bluewin.ch.

sections.heading: Fonts
body: Fonts (Instrument Serif, Inter, JetBrains Mono) are served locally from this server. There are no connections to Google Fonts or any other third-party servers.

sections.heading: External links
body: This site links to external platforms in several places, whose content I cannot influence or control. When you click an external link you leave this site, and the privacy policy of the respective provider applies from that point.

Currently linked external services: GitHub (github.com), LinkedIn (linkedin.com), Instagram (instagram.com), Adobe Portfolio (manuelheller.myportfolio.com), Twitch (twitch.tv), Jogge di Balla (joggediballa.ch). A list of the exact providers with their country of residence can be requested at any time via email.

sections.heading: Data security
body: All connections to this site are encrypted via TLS 1.3. The server is updated regularly and shielded by Cloudflare's proxy against automated attacks. These measures meet the requirements of Art. 32 GDPR and Art. 8 revFADP.

sections.heading: Your rights
body: Under the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (revFADP) and the EU GDPR you have the right to access, correction, deletion, restriction of processing, and data portability. For all enquiries write to: manuelheller@bluewin.ch.

For complaints about the processing of your data, you can contact the competent supervisory authority: in Switzerland the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC, https://www.edoeb.admin.ch). Within the EU, the data protection authority of your country of residence.

notFound.headline: This page got lost in the fluid.
notFound.body: The URL doesn't exist. Either a typo, an old link, or the ink swallowed it. Both are possible.
